proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the 80 binary patient features in ds001_aml, at least one feature has a statistically significant univariate association with objective_response.
feature_035 = 1 (vs 0) is associated with a higher rate of objective_response in this AML cohort.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square screen of all 80 binary features. Overall response rate is 16.9%. The strongest signal is feature_035 (n=3624, 7.2% prevalence): response 22.1% vs 16.5% in others (absolute diff +5.6 pp; chi-square p = 3.5e-18). Other binary features with p<0.05: feature_093 (+1.7 pp, p=0.006), feature_121 (-1.4 pp, p=0.025), feature_014 (+0.7 pp, p=0.031). The vast majority of binary features show no association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 36 continuous patient features, at least one shows mean values that differ between responders and non-responders.
feature_011 is lower in responders than in non-responders in this AML cohort.
feature_006 is lower in responders than in non-responders.

analyses.code: for c in float_cols: ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,c], df.loc[y==0,c], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test on each continuous feature. feature_011 mean is 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders (diff -0.57; t=-13.08, p=7.2e-39) — strongest continuous signal. feature_006 mean 58.2 vs 60.3 (diff -2.08; p=9.5e-20). feature_063 (5.65 vs 6.17, p=4.4e-7), feature_099 (3.82 vs 3.80, p=1.3e-6), feature_092 (19.07 vs 20.40, p=1.3e-5) are also highly significant. Most other continuous features show no association.
feature_006 mean 58.23 in responders vs 60.31 in non-responders, diff -2.08 (Welch t = -9.11, p = 9.5e-20).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal) has a monotone decreasing relationship with the probability of objective_response: each increment of feature_057 lowers the response probability.
The 5-level features (feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042, feature_125) and the 11-level feature_018 each show a linear ordinal trend with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_057 coded as ordinal predictor: beta = -0.3837 per unit increase, OR = 0.681, p = 1.8e-97. Response rates by level: 21.3% (level 0, n=17,592), 15.5% (level 1, n=24,971), 11.2% (level 2, n=7,437) — strong monotone decrease. This is the single strongest predictor in the dataset.

Linear-trend logistic for each multi-level feature. feature_096 p=0.51, feature_064 p=0.21, feature_045 p=0.07 (negative), feature_042 p=0.35, feature_125 p=0.038 (small negative beta=-0.023), feature_018 p=0.34. Only feature_125 reaches conventional significance, with a small effect. Trend hypothesis is rejected for the other multi-level features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Response rates differ across patient race groups (feature_005), with white patients having higher response than non-white patients in this AML cohort.
Response rates differ across insurance categories (feature_087: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: white 17.28% (n=32,498), hispanic 16.70% (n=7,631), asian 16.33% (n=2,970), black 15.74% (n=5,889), other 14.72% (n=1,012). Chi-square p = 0.0096. White patients have the highest response rate; black and 'other' have the lowest. The disparity (white vs black) is roughly 1.5 percentage points absolute.
Response rates by insurance: medicare 17.18%, medicaid 16.87%, private 16.66%, uninsured 16.62%. Chi-square p = 0.54 (not significant). No insurance-level disparity in observed response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, and feature_035 all remain independently associated with objective_response in a multivariable logistic regression (i.e., they capture non-redundant signal).

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression with 7 top univariate predictors (continuous standardized). All coefficients remain significant: feature_057 beta=-0.383 (p<1e-300), feature_011_z beta=-0.155 (p=1.4e-34), feature_006_z beta=-0.110 (p=7.0e-20), feature_063_z beta=-0.067 (p=1.3e-6), feature_099_z beta=+0.059 (p=9.4e-7), feature_092_z beta=-0.058 (p=1.0e-5), feature_035 beta=+0.360 (p=1.9e-17). Pseudo-R^2 = 0.018; AIC = 44,622. All seven predictors carry independent signal; none is redundant with the others.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The lower response rate among black patients (vs white) is fully explained by differences in clinical/biomarker covariates — i.e., adjusting for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_035, and feature_078 (age) eliminates the race effect.

analyses.result_summary: Race effect adjusted for 8 clinical covariates. Black race retains an independent negative association with response (beta = -0.109, OR = 0.90, p = 0.005). Asian beta=-0.079 (p=0.13), hispanic beta=-0.036 (p=0.30), 'other' beta=-0.196 (p=0.031). Hypothesis REFUTED: the black-vs-white disparity is NOT explained away by clinical covariates and remains roughly the same magnitude as the unadjusted estimate (-0.112).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding insurance category to the clinical+race-adjusted model further attenuates the residual black-vs-white response disparity (i.e., insurance partially mediates the racial gap).

analyses.result_summary: Adding insurance dummies (medicaid, medicare, uninsured; private = reference) to the clinical+race model. Black race coefficient is virtually unchanged (beta = -0.109, p = 0.005). None of the insurance coefficients is significant (medicaid p=0.59, medicare p=0.12, uninsured p=0.87). Hypothesis REFUTED: insurance does not mediate the racial gap in response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035 (binary) interacts with feature_057 (3-level ordinal) on response: the protective effect of feature_035=1 is stronger in higher feature_057 levels (or differs across them).
feature_011 (continuous) and feature_057 (ordinal) interact: the negative effect of feature_011 differs across feature_057 levels.
feature_035 and feature_011 interact: the positive effect of feature_035 is modified by feature_011 levels.

analyses.result_summary: feature_057 main effect beta=-0.385 (p<1e-50), feature_035 main effect beta=+0.340 (p=2e-8), interaction term beta=+0.027 (p=0.67). No evidence of multiplicative effect modification — feature_035 boosts response on the log-odds scale equivalently across feature_057 strata.
Interaction beta = -0.007, p = 0.71. No effect modification.
Interaction beta = -0.054, p = 0.23. No significant effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between black race and objective_response is modified by feature_057 — the racial disparity is larger (or smaller) at higher feature_057 levels.
The protective effect of feature_035 is smaller (less positive) in black patients than in white patients.
The negative effect of feature_011 on response is stronger in black patients than in non-black patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta = -0.065, p = 0.27. No statistically significant effect modification of feature_057's effect by black race.

feature_035 main beta=+0.374 (p<1e-15), black main beta=-0.089 (p=0.026), interaction beta=-0.089 (p=0.51). No significant interaction; feature_035 confers a similar log-odds boost in black and non-black patients.

Stratified univariate slopes: Black patients beta(feature_011_z) = -0.169 (p = 6.4e-6, n=5,889); non-Black patients beta = -0.153 (p < 1e-29, n=44,111). Difference is small and non-significant (interaction term p = 0.69). No meaningful effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each level of feature_057, patients with feature_035=1 have a higher absolute response rate than patients with feature_035=0.

analyses.code: stratified Logit on feature_035 within each feature_057 level
analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0: response 27.0% (feature_035=1) vs 20.8% (feature_035=0); OR=1.40, p=1.5e-7. feature_057=1: 20.5% vs 15.1%; OR=1.44, p=2.4e-9. feature_057=2: 15.3% vs 10.9%; OR=1.48, p=2.2e-3. The protective effect of feature_035 is consistent across all 3 strata of feature_057, supporting an additive log-odds model with no interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each level of feature_057, white patients have a higher response rate than black patients (the racial gap is present across all clinical risk strata).

analyses.code: stratified mean response by race within feature_057
analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0: white 21.7%, black 20.5%, hispanic 20.9%, asian 20.4%, other 17.0%. feature_057=1: white 15.8%, black 14.3%, hispanic 15.4%, asian 15.1%, other 14.9%. feature_057=2: white 11.7%, black 9.5%, hispanic 10.9%, asian 10.5%, other 8.6%. Across every stratum, black patients have a lower response rate than white patients. The gap is largest in absolute terms (~2.2 pp) at feature_057=2 (the worst-prognosis stratum), supporting a consistent racial disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The log-odds of response is non-linear (quadratic) in at least one of feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, or feature_078 (age).

analyses.code: Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[[c+'_z', c+'_z_sq']])).fit() for each c
analyses.result_summary: Quadratic terms (after centering): feature_011 sq p=0.07 (negative — slight downward curvature), feature_006 sq p=0.72 (linear), feature_063 sq p=0.06 (positive), feature_099 sq p=0.76 (linear), feature_092 sq p=2.6e-4 (POSITIVE — significant U-shape suggesting attenuation at extremes), feature_078 (age) linear p=0.77 + sq p=0.31 (no association at all). Conclusion: feature_092 has a statistically significant non-linear relationship with response; feature_078 (age) is essentially unrelated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Because feature_092 is right-skewed (range 0.5 to 500), a log transformation provides a better functional form than the raw value, with a more significant linear association with the log-odds of response.

analyses.result_summary: log(feature_092) in a 4-predictor multivariable model: beta = -0.070, p = 4.5e-9, model AIC = 44,719.9. Using raw standardized feature_092 instead: beta = -0.058, p = 1e-5, AIC = 44,734.1. The log-transformed version is a better fit by ~14 AIC points (and the coefficient z-score nearly doubles), confirming that feature_092 acts on the log scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After conditioning on the 7-predictor core model (feature_057 + feature_011_z + feature_006_z + feature_063_z + feature_099_z + feature_092_z + feature_035), additional binary features (e.g. feature_093, feature_014, feature_121) provide independent predictive signal.

analyses.code: for c in remaining_binary: LR_test(core_model + c vs core_model)
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test of each remaining binary feature against the 7-predictor core. Significant additions: feature_093 beta=+0.125 (p=0.0039), feature_014 beta=+0.059 (p=0.017), feature_121 beta=-0.088 (p=0.048), feature_012 beta=-0.048 (p=0.050). All other binary features p>0.05 after adjustment. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: a small additional set of binary features carries independent signal beyond the top 7 predictors, but the gains are modest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After conditioning on the core 7-predictor model, additional continuous features provide further independent signal — in particular feature_084, feature_028, feature_031, or feature_033.

analyses.code: for c in remaining_continuous: LR_test(core_model + c_z vs core_model)
analyses.result_summary: feature_084 beta=+0.027 (p=0.026), feature_028 beta=-0.024 (p=0.049), feature_031 beta=+0.023 (p=0.053), feature_033 beta=-0.023 (p=0.057). Only feature_084 and feature_028 reach p<0.05; the effects are very small. Most remaining continuous features (e.g., feature_044, feature_119, feature_086, feature_037, feature_106, feature_077) are essentially independent of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 (an 11-level integer 0..10) has a non-linear relationship with response — i.e., a categorical encoding fits better than a linear one.

analyses.code: Logit linear vs Logit categorical for feature_018; LR test
analyses.result_summary: Linear feature_018: beta=0.004, p=0.34, AIC=45,439. Categorical (10 dummy variables) AIC=45,451. LR chi-square = 5.89 on 9 df, p = 0.75. Hypothesis REFUTED: feature_018 is not related to response, either linearly or non-linearly. Response rates fluctuate between ~16.3% and ~19.0% across levels with no clear trend.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Quartile analysis of feature_011 reveals a monotone decreasing response rate from lowest to highest quartile.
Same monotone trend holds for feature_006, feature_092, feature_063 (response decreases with higher quartile) and the reverse for feature_099 (response increases with higher quartile).

analyses.result_summary: feature_011 is heavily zero-inflated (28% of patients = 0); only 3 distinct quartiles are produced. Response rate Q0 (n=25,138) = 18.6%, Q1 (n=12,562) = 16.8%, Q2 (n=12,300) = 13.6%. Monotone decreasing — pattern supports the hypothesis.

feature_006 quartiles: 18.96, 17.48, 16.50, 14.67% (monotone decreasing). feature_092: 18.00, 17.41, 16.57, 15.63% (monotone). feature_063: 17.32, 17.56, 17.18, 15.56% (decline only in Q4). feature_099: 15.84, 16.77, 17.60, 17.76% (monotone increasing). All consistent with the linear logistic effects estimated earlier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: log(feature_092) interacts with feature_057: the log-protective effect of feature_092 differs across feature_057 levels.

analyses.result_summary: Main effects log_092 beta=-0.044 (p=0.010) and feature_057 beta=-0.291 (p<1e-8); interaction term beta = -0.038, p = 0.038. Marginally significant negative interaction — high feature_092 has a stronger negative effect on response in patients with higher feature_057. This is the only significant pairwise interaction we identified (and it is borderline).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a significant 3-way interaction among feature_057, feature_011, and black race (i.e., the joint effect of feature_057 and feature_011 differs in black vs non-black patients).

analyses.result_summary: All interaction terms non-significant. 3-way interaction beta = +0.030, p = 0.62. The main effects model is adequate; no evidence of complex effect modification by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable logistic model combining the top 11 predictors achieves modest discrimination (AUC > 0.55) for objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: 11-predictor model (feature_057, feature_011_z, feature_006_z, log_092, feature_063_z, feature_099_z, feature_035, feature_093, feature_014, feature_084_z, black). In-sample AUC = 0.5989, pseudo-R^2 = 0.019. Discrimination is modest but reliably above chance — most of the response variance is unexplained by measured features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A clinical-only model (excluding race) is well calibrated across race groups — observed and predicted response rates agree within each race.

analyses.code: compare mean predicted vs mean observed response by race for race-blind model
analyses.result_summary: Mean observed vs predicted response by race: white 17.28% vs 16.92% (+0.36 pp), asian 16.33% vs 17.03% (-0.70 pp), black 15.74% vs 16.85% (-1.11 pp), hispanic 16.70% vs 16.83% (-0.14 pp), other 14.72% vs 16.89% (-2.17 pp). Hypothesis REFUTED: a clinical-only model SYSTEMATICALLY over-predicts response for black and 'other' patients (and slightly for asian) while it is correctly calibrated for white patients. The magnitude of mis-calibration (~6% relative for black, ~13% relative for 'other') is meaningful and aligns with the unexplained residual race effect documented in iterations 6–7.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The predictive effect of feature_011 on response is similar (parallel slopes) in black and non-black patients.

analyses.code: Logit per race group on feature_011_z
analyses.result_summary: Black patients: beta = -0.169 (p = 6.4e-6, n = 5,889). Non-black: beta = -0.153 (p < 1e-29, n = 44,111). Slopes are very close, and the interaction term in iteration 9.3 was non-significant (p = 0.69). Hypothesis SUPPORTED: feature_011 carries the same per-SD prognostic effect in both groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A simple count of 'favorable' binary features (feature_035, feature_093, feature_014) shows a strong positive dose-response with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate by fav_count: 0 features = 16.45% (n=25,575), 1 feature = 16.47% (n=21,281), 2 features = 23.26% (n=3,044), 3 features = 33.0% (n=100). The dose-response is non-linear: only patients with 2 or 3 favorable markers get a meaningful response boost. This is consistent with feature_035 being the dominant signal among the three.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding log(feature_092) and feature_084 to the 7-predictor core, plus the binary features feature_093, feature_014, and a black-race indicator, produces a final model with statistically significant terms for all retained predictors and improved fit (AIC < 44,610).

analyses.result_summary: Final model coefficients (all on log-odds scale): feature_057 -0.385 (p<1e-90), feature_011_z -0.156 (p=1e-34), feature_006_z -0.110 (p=7e-20), feature_063_z -0.067 (p=1e-6), feature_099_z +0.059 (p=1e-6), log(feature_092) -0.071 (p=4e-9), feature_035 +0.360 (p=2e-17), feature_093 +0.124 (p=0.004), feature_014 +0.058 (p=0.018), feature_084_z +0.027 (p=0.024), black -0.094 (p=0.014). AIC = 44,591.4 — improvement of ~31 over the 7-predictor core (44,622). All 11 terms remain significant; the model is parsimonious. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the entire feature set, the strongest treatment-/biomarker-relevant signals for objective_response are: feature_057 (3-level ordinal, monotone decreasing), feature_011 (continuous, lower=responder), feature_006 (continuous, lower=responder), feature_092 (continuous, log-acting, lower=responder), feature_099 (continuous, higher=responder), and feature_035 (binary, present=responder); with a residual demographic disparity (black race) that is independent of measured covariates.
Most of the candidate features (the great majority of binary, multi-level ordinal, and continuous variables) carry no detectable association with objective_response in this cohort.

analyses.code: summary of cumulative findings from iterations 1-24
analyses.result_summary: Synthesizing all preceding iterations: six independent biomarker/clinical features (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, log feature_092, feature_099, feature_035) account for almost all the signal in objective_response. feature_057 alone is the most powerful (OR=0.68 per level, p<1e-90). Pairwise and 3-way interactions among these features are largely absent (only log_092 x feature_057 reaches p=0.038). After full adjustment, black race retains an OR of approximately 0.91 (p=0.014), and the predictive model under-predicts non-response in black and 'other' patients by ~1-2 percentage points. The full 11-predictor model has AIC 44,591 and AUC 0.60. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: count of features with FDR-corrected p>0.05
analyses.result_summary: Of the 80 binary features, only 4 are univariately significant at p<0.05 (feature_035, feature_093, feature_121, feature_014) and only 3 of those add independent signal to the core model. Of the 36 continuous features, 5 are strongly significant (feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092) and 2 more (feature_084, feature_028) are weakly significant after adjustment. Of the 6 multi-level ordinal features, only feature_057 has a meaningful association; feature_125 has a borderline trend; the rest (feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042, feature_018) are null. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: the vast majority of features in this de-identified cohort have no detectable relationship with objective_response.
